name: FullStory
what_it_does: Digital experience analytics platform API for accessing session recording data, custom event data, user behavioral analytics, and frustration signals (rage clicks, dead clicks) to understand how users interact with products.
use_cases: Enriching customer support data with session recordings — linking support tickets to user sessions
Querying FullStory event data for behavioral cohort analysis in agent-driven research
Exporting user sessions and events for custom analytics pipelines
Triggering in-product surveys or messages based on FullStory-detected frustration signals
Building custom dashboards over FullStory behavioral data for product intelligence agents
not_for: Primary analytics (use Google Analytics or Mixpanel for event analytics)
A/B testing and experimentation (use LaunchDarkly or Optimizely)
Performance monitoring (use Datadog or New Relic)
Cost-sensitive startups — FullStory is an enterprise product with enterprise pricing
best_when: You need to correlate session recordings with business events, enrich customer support or CRM data with behavioral context, or build custom queries over rich user behavior data.
avoid_when: You're looking for a free or affordable analytics tool — FullStory is enterprise-priced and overkill for simple analytics needs.

auth.notes: API key in Authorization header. Keys available from FullStory admin settings. No scoping — key has full access to organization's data.

pricing.paid_tiers: Business from $199/month; Enterprise with custom pricing based on sessions
pricing.notes: Enterprise product with no meaningful free tier. Pricing based on monthly sessions captured. API access available on paid plans.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits exist but not publicly documented; enterprise plans have higher limits

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API documentation is reasonable. Error responses follow standard patterns. Some endpoints have limited documentation around edge cases.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Data export operations are read-only — fully idempotent. Event creation (server-side events) is not idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Session recording URLs expire — agents accessing session links must do so promptly after retrieval
API is designed for data export, not real-time querying — high-frequency polling is not suitable
Privacy regulations mean some user data may be masked or excluded from API responses
FullStory Behavioral Data Layer (custom events) requires client-side instrumentation setup first
Annual contract required — significant commitment before evaluating API capabilities
Data retention periods limit historical data available via API

security_score_components.security_notes: Session recording platform — contains user behavior data. API key auth. GDPR/CCPA data deletion APIs available. Privacy controls for PII masking.
